§1 — The Origin Story

Sherman & Marian McClellan · 1969 · And Why This Indicator Still Matters

Before we look at today's market, a brief but important history — because understanding who created this tool and why helps you respect what it is telling you right now.

📺 A Personal Memory — Gene Morgan's FNN Show · Los Angeles · 1980

In 1980, Dr. UV watched Sherman and Marian McClellan on Gene Morgan's afternoon market show on FNN (Financial News Network) — the predecessor to CNBC — broadcast daily from 1:00 to 1:30 PM after the market close in Los Angeles. The McClellan Oscillator was already over a decade old by then, and its creators were explaining breadth analysis to retail investors years before it became mainstream.

FNN was later acquired by CNBC. Gene Morgan's show was one of the first daily market programs for individual investors. The McClellan family was there from the beginning — teaching the math of market breadth when almost no one else was talking about it.

Today, Tom McClellan — Sherman and Marian's son — continues that work through McClellan Financial Publications. The oscillator his parents created in 1969 remains one of the most respected breadth indicators in technical analysis. Forty-six years of watching this tool. The signals have not changed. The math has not changed. And right now, the math is speaking clearly.

📐 What the McClellan Oscillator Actually Measures

The core concept: Price is what you see. Breadth is what is actually happening underneath. The McClellan Oscillator measures the difference between two exponential moving averages of NYSE advancing issues minus declining issues (the A-D data). It is a momentum measure of market breadth — not price.

Why divergence matters: When price makes new highs but the McClellan Oscillator is falling or negative — that is a breadth divergence. It means fewer and fewer stocks are participating in the advance. The index is being held up by a shrinking number of leaders. This is not sustainable. In Dr. UV's observation over 45+ years: 90% of the time, a sustained NYSE MO divergence manifests in at least a short-term market decline. It does not tell you the exact timeframe — but it tells you the direction.

Current reading (June 4, 2026): NYSE McClellan Oscillator: −6.465. The oscillator has been below zero since May 11 — 3+ weeks of negative breadth while price made new highs. The Summation Index (+1,697) is now falling. The RASI has stalled at +500. Today the divergence is resolving — to the downside.

§2 — The Warning Timeline

Dr. UV Was Saying This For Over a Week

These are the documented warnings — posted publicly before today's breakdown. The McClellan Oscillator divergence and UVCMI 777™ multi-timeframe deterioration were visible well in advance.

⚠️ DR. UV WARNING TIMELINE — NYSE MO DIVERGENCE
May 11+
NYSE McClellan Oscillator drops below zero. Price continues making highs. Classic breadth divergence begins. UVCMI 777™ begins showing deterioration in shorter timeframes across master tracker components.
Jun 2
Crypto continues to crater. "Consistent warnings here. Coupled with all-timeframe sells in UVCMI 777™ of Crypto tracker. Equities showing cracks in lower timeframes. Continued breadth divergence from McClellan oscillator. Shows bull is vulnerable anytime."
Jun 3
NYSE MO reading: −4.25 in Master Tracker post. SPY at $759 — still near highs. UVCMI 777™ W readings begin showing stress. "NYSE MO diverging from price highs — KEY WARNING SIGNAL" noted in session context.
Jun 4 AM
"Equity indexes started cracking. Nasdaq finally pulling back. Watch VXX — ST buy will tilt the odds to the bears. Crypto entering deep red territory. PM choppy with strong bearish bias. Strong divergence of McClellan oscillator. Should be cause of worry for equity bulls."
Jun 4 PM
"Time for the tide to turn back. SPLV vs SPHB." Chart showing High Beta ETF (SPHB) +125% since Liberation Day vs Low Vol ETF (SPLV) flat. Rotation warning: tide turning from high-beta to defensive.
Jun 5 NOW
CONFIRMED: SPY −1.39% · QQQ −2.66% · SOXX −6.09% · ETHE −11.02% · WGMI −11.44% · VXX +3.70%. NYSE MO: −18.61. The divergence has resolved. The warnings were right.

§5 — UVCMI 20/20 Vision™

20 Timeframes Simultaneously — Broad Deterioration Exposed

Dr. UV's proprietary UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ view shows all master tracker components across 20 timeframes at once. This is not a standard view — it is a custom diagnostic tool for gauging the true breadth of momentum. Today it confirms what the NYSE MO has been warning.

UVCMI 20/20 Vision™

DR. UV'S CUSTOM MULTI-TIMEFRAME DIAGNOSTIC · NOT SHOWN IN STANDARD TRACKER VIEWS

The standard UVCMI 777™ runs 7 timeframes. The 20/20 Vision™ expands this to 20 simultaneous timeframe reads — from 5-minute to weekly — giving a complete panoramic view of momentum across every relevant horizon. When this view shows broad red across most instruments and most timeframes simultaneously, it is one of the strongest warning signals in the system.

Today's 20/20 Vision™ screen shows orange crossing lines across multiple asset classes — the visual signature of a regime change. Assets that were trending upward across multiple timeframes are now crossing into negative territory simultaneously. This cross-asset, cross-timeframe deterioration is the confirmation that the NYSE MO divergence warned about.

UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ · Dr. UV's Master Tracker · 20 Simultaneous Timeframes · Orange crossing lines = regime change signature · Cross-asset deterioration confirmed · June 5, 2026

📊 20/20 Vision™ Read — What the Orange X Pattern Means

The crossing lines: Each orange line represents a UVCMI trend vector for a major asset class. When lines cross — positive going to negative, negative going to positive — it marks a momentum transition. Multiple simultaneous crossings across unrelated asset classes (equities, crypto, precious metals, energy) is a systemic signal, not sector-specific noise.

What is crossing DOWN: SOXX (semis), QQQ (tech), SPY (broad market), GLD (gold), SLV (silver), GDX/GDXJ (miners), GBTC/ETHE (crypto) — all major sectors crossing into negative UVCMI territory simultaneously.

What is crossing UP: VXX (volatility). That is the only vector moving in the other direction. When the sole upward cross is volatility, the message is unambiguous.

SOXX — the Lead Scout has fallen: SOXX was the last major positive UVCMI cluster. In Post #568, SOXX was identified as the Lead Scout — the sector that signals QQQ direction in advance. When SOXX turns negative with this magnitude (−6.09% today), it is not a warning — it is confirmation that the broader tech complex will follow.

Dr. UV's Master Tracker · IT-LT Screen · D/2D/3D/W UVCMI · Orange crossing lines confirm multi-week deterioration · Sorted GDX · June 5, 2026

📊 Full Screen Read — UVCMI 777™ Confirms Across All Timeframes

B Screen (Year/Quarter/Month/Week/Day): VXX is the only instrument with a positive short-term B1 reading today (+3.70%). Every other major asset — equities, crypto, precious metals, energy — is negative on the day. The weekly B1 readings (W column) are now turning negative for previously-strong names. This is the transition from "short-term weakness" to "intermediate-term trend change."

ST Screen (1H/2H/4H): SPY 1H: −194 · QQQ 1H: −292 · SOXX 1H: −346. All deeply negative and accelerating. No intraday recovery attempts holding. This is the signature of genuine selling pressure — not profit-taking dips that recover within hours.

IT-LT Screen (D/2D/3D/W): The orange crossing lines tell the story — weekly UVCMI readings that were positive are being consumed by daily deterioration. ETHE D: −3,807 · GBTC D: −1,857 · SOXX showing positive W (+2,460) being overwhelmed by negative D/2D. When the daily overrides the weekly, the trend is changing — not just correcting.

The Safe Harbor signal from Post #568: VXX ST buy confirmed. The "2-Step Exit" rule — 50% reduction when 1H/4H diverge, full exit when 1D breaks — is now fully triggered for most long positions across the master tracker universe.

§7 — What Comes Next

The NYSE MO Doesn't Give You Timing — It Gives You Direction

🎯 UVCMI 777™ Current Assessment — June 5, 2026

Direction confirmed: Down. NYSE MO −18.61 · UVCMI 777™ negative across ST and beginning to consume IT-LT weekly readings · SOXX (Lead Scout) broken · $750 SPY support broken · VXX ST buy active.

What MO divergence tells us: 90% of sustained divergences (MO negative while price at highs) resolve in at least a short-term decline. The "at least" is the key phrase — it can be a 3-5% correction or the beginning of something larger. The MO does not give you the magnitude or the exact timing. It gives you the direction and the probability.

Watch for recovery signals: NYSE MO recrossing above zero AND UVCMI 777™ short-term frames turning green simultaneously = potential re-entry. Until both conditions are met, the mathematical edge favors bears or cash.

Safe Harbor per UVCMI 777™ Post #568: When SPY/QQQ pillars turn red, the 777 Radar searches for defensive structural resonance in XLU (Utilities) or XLV (Healthcare). These are currently showing relative strength vs. the broader selling. UUP (Dollar) +0.63% also confirms flight to safety.

DR. UV's World Asset Tracker™ · B-Screen™ · RelS vs SPY™ · DR UVC Universal™
UVCMI 777™ is Dr. UV's Master Indicator calculated from a single proprietary formula incorporating the last 60 units of closing prices. It runs across 7 timeframes simultaneously (1H, 2H, 4H, D, 2D, 3D, W). UVCMI 20/20 Vision™ is a proprietary extended view running 20 simultaneous timeframes for broad market diagnostic purposes. Green values = positive momentum. Red values = negative momentum. The NYSE McClellan Oscillator is cited with full credit and appreciation to McClellan Financial Publications (mcoscillator.com) — created 1969 by Sherman and Marian McClellan.
